summary:
Vitamin K is crucial for activating coagulation factors via gamma-carboxylation, particularly enabling prothrombin to bind calcium and form effective clots. The most accurately supported clinical point among available options is its role in facilitating prothrombin production.  

\boxed{\text{A}}
